user.title: Repository Settings
user.description:
Configure the git repository for config synchronisation.

SSH key is only required if you want to PUSH changes back to the remote. You can leave it blank for read-only (pull only) access.

data.ssh_key_path: SSH private key path (optional, for push)

init_git.title: Initialize Git Repository?
init_git.description: The directory at '{path}' is not a git repository yet. Would you like to initialize it now? This will create a .git directory and configure git with default settings. You can also skip this and set it up manually later.

settings.title: Notification & Commit Settings
settings.description: Configure git operations and how you are notified.

data.ssh_key_path: SSH private key path (optional, for push)

error.git_not_found: git binary not found in the HA container
error.git_init_failed: Failed to initialize git repository. Check that the directory is writable.
error.ssh_key_not_found: SSH key file not found at the specified path. Generate one with: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /config/.ssh/id_ed25519. Or leave blank for read-only (pull-only) access.
